# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment that pulls our application image from Docker Hub. Use the latest tag so we always get the newest version automatically. This is for our development environment where we want continuous updates without manual intervention.

Prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app-deployment
  labels:
    app: myapp
    environment: development
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: myapp
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: myapp
        environment: development
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: myapp-container
        image: dockerhub-username/myapp:latest
        imagePullPolicy: Always
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080
        securityContext:
          runAsNonRoot: true
          runAsUser: 1000
          allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
          readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
          capabilities:
            drop:
            - ALL
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "128Mi"
            cpu: "100m"
          limits:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "200m"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: tmp
          mountPath: /tmp
        - name: cache
          mountPath: /app/cache
      securityContext:
        fsGroup: 1000
        seccompProfile:
          type: RuntimeDefault
      volumes:
      - name: tmp
        emptyDir: {}
      - name: cache
        emptyDir: {}
